Additional Notes
REFERRAL TO ENT [1208418751]
a 23 year old female with of recurrent pharyngitis, including a significant episode four
years ago requiring 3 course Augementin and 1 dose of Rocephin IM. Was offered a referral to ENT but she
did not seek ENT consultation as symptoms resolved. One year ago, she observed a stable white "polyp on
her tonsil" with no pain or discomfort, only occasional foreign body sensation.
Reason for Referral: Evaluation and treatment of cyst like structure on left tonsil
Referring to (location): Location covered by patient's insurance

Progress Notes by Moises Aranda, PA-C at
Jenevieve or guardian(s), if you are reviewing this progress note and have questions about the meaning or
medical terms being used, please schedule an appointment or bring it up at your next follow-up
appointment. Medical notes are a communication tool between medical professionals and require medical
terms to be used for efficiency.
CommuniCare OLE
CCOLE Hansen Family Health Center Location
215 West Beamer St
Woodland CA 95695-2510
Phone: 530-405-2800
Fax: 530-204-5295
Date:
Interpreter Services Used?: No. Provider speaks same language as patient - English.
Chief Complaint
Patient presents with
Pharyngitis
Pt presents to clinic following upon pharyngitis she has been battling. Pt states she is still not
feeling well. Pt still having tonsil pain, discharge, she feels like she is swallowing glass, stiff neck
and random fevers. Pt seen at urgent care on Monday and was given penecillin
Subjective
History of Present Illness
As
€
presents for evaluation of tonsillitis. She was previously referred to the ER, where a CT
scan showed no masses but indicated a possible tonsil stone. She was prescribed steroids to address
inflammation and pus. Her symptoms worsened on Sunday, with her tonsils swelling to the size of "golf balls"
and becoming covered in pus. Although the swelling has since subsided, she continues to experience a sore
throat. She has been on penicillin for four days, which has been less effective than previous antibiotics. She
still has white pus on her tonsils but has not experienced any rashes or fevers above 99°F. She reports neck
soreness, a headache behind her right eye, ear pain, and ringing in her ears. Advil has been effective in
managing her pain.
She has not engaged in oral or vaginal sex for the past four years. Both Monospot and strep tests have
returned negative results. She does not have a cough and attributes her fatigue to a lack of energy drinks.
She is overdue for varicella, influenza, and Tdap vaccines. She is also due for a Pap smear. She is employed
at UC Davis and consumes energy drinks.
No past medical history on file.
No past surgical history on file.

Progress Notes by Moises Aranda, PA-C aM
COMPARISON: None
TECHNIQUE:
and coronal reformats.
Helical post-contrast CT images of the neck were obtained from the skull base to the lung apices with sagittal
Contrast: 100 CC Omnipaque 350 was administered intravenously.
Total exam Dose Length Product for a 425 mGy-cm
Total exam CT Dose Index 13 mGy
FINDINGS:
Nasopharynx: Unremarkable.
Oropharynx and hypopharynx: Mild swelling of the oropharyngeal tonsils. Mild associated hyperemia, more
notable on the left. Suspect trapped secretions in a left tonsillar crypt.
Larynx: Unremarkable.
Parotid glands: Unremarkable.
Submandibular glands: Unremarkable.
Thyroid gland: Unremarkable.
Lymph nodes: Mild hyperemic lymphadenopathy left neck.
Vascular structures: Visualized portions unremarkable.
Soft tissues including subcutaneous tissue: [Retropharyngeal space preserved.
Osseous structures: Degenerative changes of the TMJ with flattened mandibular condyles bilaterally. Reversal
cervical spine.
of the normal lordosis of the cervical spine. No canal stenosis or significant neuroforamina narrowing of the
Trachea and airway: Airway patent.
Lung apices: Visualized portion clear.
Other: Mild mucosal thickening of the paranasal sinuses. Mucous retention cyst versus polyp in the right
maxillary sinus. Mastoid air cells are clear. Partially empty sella.
IMPRESSION:
Mild swelling of the oropharyngeal tonsils. Mild associated hyperemia, more notable on the left.
Suspect trapped secretions in a left tonsillar crypt. Direct inspection recommended.
Mild hyperemic lymphadenopathy in the left neck.
Assessment and Plan
Assessment & Plan
1. Tonsillitis being treating empirically as strep despite negative POCT strep tests.
- Symptoms: tonsils_with exudates, sore throat, headache, B/L ear pain.
- Some improvement, exudates persist.
- Continue 10-day penicillin course.
- Alternate Advil and Tylenol for pain.
- Throat swab for chlamydia/gonorrhea given history of oral sex in the past.
- Blood work for mononucleosis.
- Referred to ENT for possible tonsil stone/polyp like structure on left tonsil X 1 year.
2. Health maintenance.

claude_vision.referral_reason: Evaluation and treatment of a cyst-like structure / polyp on the left tonsil that has been present for approximately 1 year, in the context of recurrent pharyngitis and an acute episode of severe tonsillitis with bilateral tonsillar exudates. CT neck findings suggest trapped secretions in a left tonsillar crypt with mild oropharyngeal tonsil swelling and left neck lymphadenopathy.
relevant_clinical_findings: 23-year-old female with history of recurrent pharyngitis, including a significant episode 4 years ago requiring 3 courses of Augmentin and 1 dose of Rocephin IM
Approximately 1 year ago, patient observed a stable white 'polyp on her tonsil' with no pain or discomfort, only occasional foreign body sensation
Current acute episode: tonsil pain, discharge, sensation of swallowing glass, stiff neck, random fevers, bilateral ear pain, headache behind right eye, tinnitus
Tonsils swelled to 'golf ball' size with pus; swelling has since partially subsided but white pus and exudates persist
Seen at urgent care and given penicillin; has been on penicillin 4 days with less effectiveness than prior antibiotics
Monospot and strep tests negative (POCT); empirically treated as strep tonsillitis
Physical exam: tonsillar exudate present, 1+ on right and 1+ on left; bilateral superficial cervical adenopathy; no acute distress

CT neck impression: mild swelling of oropharyngeal tonsils more notable on left, suspected trapped secretions in left tonsillar crypt, mild hyperemic lymphadenopathy in left neck — direct inspection recommended
No rashes or fevers above 99°F at time of visit
Vitals: BP 126/84, Pulse 80, Temp 97.6°F, SpO2 99%, BMI 24.57
claude_vision.imaging_summary: CT Neck with Contrast performed (date partially redacted, referenced in progress note dated 2/19/2026): FINDINGS — Mild swelling of oropharyngeal tonsils, more notable on the left; mild associated hyperemia; suspected trapped secretions in a left tonsillar crypt; mild hyperemic lymphadenopathy in the left neck; mild mucosal thickening of paranasal sinuses; mucous retention cyst vs. polyp in right maxillary sinus; degenerative changes of the TMJ bilaterally; reversal of normal cervical lordosis; airway patent; retropharyngeal space preserved. IMPRESSION: Mild oropharyngeal tonsillar swelling left greater than right; suspected trapped secretions left tonsillar crypt — direct inspection recommended; mild hyperemic lymphadenopathy left neck. Full CT report text is included within the referring provider's progress note (pages 9–10 of fax).
missing_information: CT imaging report is embedded within the PA's progress note rather than submitted as a standalone radiology report — the original radiology report with radiologist signature is not separately attached
Lab results (monospot, strep, throat swab for chlamydia/gonorrhea, blood work for mononucleosis, hepatitis C, syphilis, HIV) ordered but results not included in fax
No prior ENT consultation notes (patient previously declined ENT referral when symptoms resolved 4 years ago)
Insurance/Medicaid ID fields on referral form are partially redacted/illegible
Completed first ENT visit (3/2/26 with Dr. Hasday — marked 'Completed') but no ENT consultation note or findings from that visit are included in this referral packet

provider_urgency_label.label: urgent
provider_urgency_label.source: Multiple locations: (1) Referral form header on CommuniCare+OLE fax (page 2 of 12): 'Urgent' listed as referral type/priority. (2) Referral Information section of Sutter Health referral record: 'Status Reason: Received Carrier Authorization' and referral note file name includes 'URG' — 'Document on 2/23/2026 1511 by Herrera-Vega, Cecilia: URG'. (3) Insurance authorization (NIVANO, page 5 of fax): 'Priority Status: 1 - STAT'. (4) CommuniCare+OLE referral form (page 2): 'Priority: Urgent [2]'. (5) Referral notes on Sutter scheduling page reference 'urg ref expo' in message thread.

criteria_check.provider_label: Urgent / STAT — marked in multiple locations: referral form header ('Urgent'), insurance authorization priority ('1 - STAT'), internal referral notes tagged 'URG'
criteria_check.reasoning: No defined ENT urgent criteria are met: there is no confirmed or suspected malignancy, no rapidly growing lesion (the tonsillar polyp/cyst-like structure has been stable for approximately 1 year), no acute nasal fracture within the surgical window, no sudden hearing loss, no airway compromise (SpO2 99%, airway patent on CT, no stridor), no infant tongue tie, no peritonsillar abscess documented, and no foreign body — however, the referring provider and insurance authorization both independently labeled this referral as Urgent/STAT, so secondary review is required to determine whether the provider has additional clinical concern not fully captured in the documentation (e.g., concern for abscess formation, malignancy, or worsening obstruction) before accepting or downgrading the urgency designation.

claude_vision.next_steps: Secondary review needed — referring provider and insurance authorization both independently labeled this referral Urgent/STAT, but no ENT urgent criteria are clinically matched; triage team should contact the referring provider (Moises Aranda, PA-C, 530-405-2800) to clarify the basis for the urgent designation before scheduling, and should also note that one visit with Dr. Hasday (3/2/26) has already been completed — obtain that consultation note prior to scheduling any follow-up.
claude_vision.summary: This is a referral for a 23-year-old female with recurrent pharyngitis and an acute severe tonsillitis episode, referred to Sacramento ENT for evaluation of a cyst-like structure / polyp on the left tonsil present for approximately 1 year. CT neck showed mild oropharyngeal tonsillar swelling left greater than right with suspected trapped secretions in the left tonsillar crypt and mild left neck lymphadenopathy, with direct inspection recommended by radiology. The referring provider and insurance authorization labeled this Urgent/STAT, but no defined ENT urgent clinical criteria (such as peritonsillar abscess, airway compromise, or malignancy) are clearly met in the documentation, and an initial ENT visit on 3/2/26 with Dr. Hasday is already marked as completed — secondary triage review is needed to clarify urgency and obtain the ENT consultation note from that visit.
